meta.title: The Last Clean Take
meta.act_label: Act 1 — The Wrong Kind of Quiet
meta.logline: A celebrated audiobook narrator, Della Voss, is found dead in her home studio the night before her biggest release. Everyone is grieving the new recordings playing at her memorial — but you knew that voice through the worst year of your life, and tonight it is wrong. Too even. Breaths like a metronome. Find out why, before they bury the truth with her.
meta.protagonist_note: In your worst year, her voice kept you company. Now you're not sure you can trust your own ear.

coldOpen.text: For eighteen months, you fell asleep to her voice. Della Voss read you through the worst year of your life.
Tonight the city is mourning her. And on every speaker, a brand-new recording plays.
Too perfect. The breaths fall like a metronome.

beats.act_label: Act 1 · The Wrong Kind of Quiet
beats.title: Saltwire Audio
beats.body:
Rain on the steps. Your badge chirps green at the door and — old habit now — you flinch.

Upstairs, a wake. Della Voss, found in her own booth last night. “She just dropped,” a constable murmurs. “Heart, probably. Peaceful.”

Peaceful. You hold onto that word the way you hold your breath. Then you go to see for yourself.
beats.callout: Open the Locations tab, enter the vocal booth, and tap the reader's chair to examine it.
advise: Start in the booth. The chair is where they found her.

beats.title: The ear
beats.body:
In the lounge, the mourners play her “new” work. The room weeps. You go still.

You know this voice. You know it better than your own. And something in it is wrong — too even, the breaths landing on a beat, like a clock pretending to be a person.

Maybe it's grief. Maybe it's your bad ear, finally lying to you. Or maybe a frightened young woman by the door has noticed the same thing.
beats.callout: Open the People tab and interview Junie. Try the ⚡ pointed question.
advise: Junie hears it too. There's a way to see the wrongness, not just hear it.

beats.title: The body isn't so sure
beats.body:
The medical examiner catches you at the door and presses a torn page into your hand. She doesn't like what she's reading.

The scene says Della died here, last night, in her chair. The note says the body disagrees.
beats.callout: Open the Evidence tab, examine the ME field note, then press + Add to board to keep the clue.
advise: If the time of death won't fit the scene, the scene is a lie.

beats.title: Look at the breathing
beats.body:
You can't unhear it, so you decide to look at it. You pull the brand-new release and an archival master from last year and lay their waveforms side by side.

A living reader breathes when their body demands it — ragged, human, never the same twice. A machine breathes on schedule.
beats.callout: In Evidence, open “The new release vs. an archival master”, study the two waveforms, and flag the one that's wrong.
advise: The new clip's breaths are evenly spaced, with a constant hum beneath. No lungs do that.

beats.title: Everyone who loved her
beats.body: They orbit the booth like planets around a dead star. Theo, her engineer, says she's “finally resting” — that she worked herself to the bone finishing the series. Priya, her agent, talks about the “biggest launch of her career” and won't touch the contract. And the man who scheduled this whole memorial-premiere, Gareth Vale, is conveniently abroad — with the clock he set ticking down over your head.
beats.callout: Interview Theo (and the others, if you like). Keep an eye on the clock up top.
advise: Theo says she was 'finishing the series.' Finishing it — how, if she's gone?

beats.title: Make the connection
beats.body: Back in the dark of your car, you lay the night out in front of you. Loose threads. A feeling. Feelings aren't evidence — but two facts that touch, that's something you can stand on.
beats.callout: Open the Board. Select two clues and press Connect. A hunch that isn't supported won't link — try until something does.
advise: A posed body and a time of death that won't fit the room — connect those two.

beats.title: Your call
beats.body:
The brass want this closed clean: a sick woman, a quiet heart, a tidy certificate before the premiere. All you have is a wrongness in your ear and a body that won't keep its story straight.

This is yours to decide.

choices.label: Hold the scene as suspicious
choices.detail: Trust the wrongness. It will cost you goodwill — and you can't fully explain it yet.
advise: The Lead held the line. Good. Now we have to earn it.

choices.label: Release it as a natural death
choices.detail: Cleaner. She was ill; the world will accept it. But you'll have to live with the breaths.
advise: We let it go — but Junie's words won't let us.
